# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #645 | surfaces: ship a first-principles axiom layer to consumers — canonical axiom block, rule-gap tie-breaker protocol, tighten-only boundary | open | — | The design axioms (accuracy > automation > cheapest-sufficient; escalate values not facts; own your own verdicts) live only in this repo's CLAUDE.md — no consumer-shipped surface carries them (audit @ 2c45d5ba: commands/agents hold only local precedence ladders; the workflow-init template lacks the hierarchy), so an agent hitting a rule gap guesses by nearest-rule pattern-match, unauditably. Proposed three-layer design: Layer 0 canonical five-axiom block (ONE file, byte-identical across editions per the adaptive-schema pattern, drift-guarded in the walkthrough); Layer 1 existing rules unchanged (new-rule discipline: must trace to an axiom); Layer 2 empirical constraints protected from principle-pruning. Only behavioral change: rule-gap tie-breaker — resolve by axiom order + record a one-line derivation in .cache evidence (adversary-auditable; recurring derivations become rule candidates). Hard boundary: tighten-only — an axiom can never be cited to skip a typed gate/refusal/barrier. Ships as prose + drift guard only; no scheduler/validator/gate changes. Six routing surfaces + workflow-init template + #307 four-chain; opencode additive. Filed by user 2026-07-08; full design on GitHub #645. |
| #646 | scout: govern the issue-scout model tier — higher-profile reasoning variant + explicit dispatch-side model wiring (close the inherit gap) | open | — | issue-scout's tier is sonnet in all three intent surfaces (source frontmatter, resolver DEFAULT_AGENT_MODELS, install manifest) with no agents/profiles/higher/ variant — AND the effective dispatch is ungoverned: install.sh rewrites frontmatter to model:inherit, the scout is not a plan node (next-action.js never resolves it), and ISSUE_SCOUT_MODEL is absent from the render-placeholder + resolve_agent_model_for_install lists, so the router dispatch carries no model= at all (manifest entry consumed by nothing). Evidence for the raise: #524 live frontier mis-rankings on the standard tier; since #545 the scout is the SOLE backlog reader with no post-dominating verifier; whole-run blast radius at once-per-run cost (planner-class shape). Fix: (1) agents/profiles/higher/issue-scout.md with model:opus (common profile keeps sonnet; resolver default unchanged); (2) ISSUE_SCOUT_MODEL placeholder + install-rendered model= in the workflow-next scout dispatch prose across the SIX routing surfaces (#400), validator-pinned; (3) NOT a REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES entry. Codex TOML twins carry no model line (prose-only there). #307 four-chain. Filed by agent (user-approved) 2026-07-09; full design on GitHub #646. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
